summary: Retest-only iteration after main-agent fix to testOpenRouterKey (now hits GET /auth/key instead of public /models). Verified 3 of 4 retest items PASS and 1 PARTIAL. Critical OpenRouter-key-validation bug from iteration 1 is FIXED. Ollama invalid-endpoint validation also works as expected. Group-project creation testIDs are wired and the project is created + appears in the sidebar, but clicking the newly-created project does NOT render a group-chat main view (no chat-input testID present after selecting the project), so the in-app send of HELLO_IN_GROUP into the group could not be exercised end-to-end. Red's 1-on-1 remains clean (empty state, no leakage), which is the correct behavior; the underlying asymmetric-memory logic was already code-reviewed as correct in iteration 1.

ui_bugs.issue: After creating a group project via the sidebar's 'New Group Project' modal, clicking 'TestProj1' in the sidebar's CURRENT PROJECTS list does not render the project's chat view in the main area. The chat-input testID is not found (count=0) and the main area continues to show the empty 'Colour Ceauxdid / Create Your First Project' landing state. This blocks the end-to-end group→1-on-1 memory-separation test because HELLO_IN_GROUP cannot actually be sent inside the group. Logic in buildContextForAgent looks correct per iteration 1 code review; the gap is purely the UI routing from sidebar.onSelectProject → main area rendering a project chat with input.

retest_1_openrouter_invalid_key.evidence: Console captured 401 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/auth/key; modal stayed open; cc_api_keys in localStorage remained null; OPENROUTER KEYS counter stays at (0). Screenshot .screenshots/r_01.png shows modal still visible with the invalid key still typed.

retest_2_ollama_invalid_endpoint.evidence: Modal stayed open after clicking Test & Save with 'http://127.0.0.1:99999'; cc_api_keys still null; OLLAMA ENDPOINTS stays at (0). Screenshot .screenshots/r_02.png shows the Ollama modal still visible with the bogus URL.

retest_3_group_project_creation_and_memo.creation: PASS — testIDs new-group-project-button, new-project-name-input, create-project-confirm all work. TestProj1 appears in the sidebar's CURRENT PROJECTS list after create.
retest_3_group_project_creation_and_memo.send_message_in_group: FAIL — clicking 'TestProj1' in sidebar does not open a chat view; chat-input is not rendered, so HELLO_IN_GROUP cannot be entered in the group.
retest_3_group_project_creation_and_memo.leak_check_red_1_on_1: PASS (vacuous) — HELLO_IN_GROUP not visible in Red's screen (count=0) and cc_msgs_agent_red is null. Because no group message was actually sent, this does not fully exercise the asymmetric-memory path but confirms no accidental leakage into the 1-on-1 scope.

retest_4_no_leftover_key_from_retest_1.evidence: Final cc_api_keys in localStorage = null; settings shows OPENROUTER KEYS (0) and OLLAMA ENDPOINTS (0).

verified_working_this_iteration: testOpenRouterKey now hits /auth/key and correctly rejects invalid keys (401).
testOllamaEndpoint returns unreachable and blocks save for bad base URLs.
SidebarNavigation create-project modal and its testIDs (new-group-project-button, new-project-name-input, create-project-confirm) all function.
Project metadata is persisted to storage and rendered in the sidebar's CURRENT PROJECTS list.
action_items: HIGH: Wire up sidebar project click → main area group chat rendering. After onSelectProject(id), ChatHub / ChatMainArea should mount a group-chat scope view that exposes testID 'chat-input' and 'chat-send-button' (same testIDs used in 1-on-1). Without this, group messages cannot be authored at all, which also blocks the asymmetric-memory feature from being observable end-to-end.

LOW (UX): The Alert.alert failure toasts on react-native-web render as browser window.alert dialogs. They worked functionally (modal stayed open, no key saved) but were not captured by the test's dialog handler in all cases — consider converting the failure feedback to an inline <Text> error in the modal for better UX and easier automated verification.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: testOpenRouterKey in /app/frontend/src/utils/api.ts now uses /auth/key — good.

GAP: the sidebar's handleCreateProject calls onSelectProject(newProject.id) but the consuming screen (ChatHub) does not appear to render a chat surface for the selected project (no chat-input after select). Either the scope wiring (project id → ChatScope) or the conditional rendering of the group chat view is missing/buggy.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (retest 1,2,4 all PASS; retest 3 partial — creation works, in-group send blocked by missing project-view routing)

test_credentials: N/A — BYOK localStorage app; invalid test values only.
seed_data_creation: localStorage was cleared before each test. One group project 'TestProj1' was created but has no messages (since chat-input wasn't rendered). No API keys persisted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the project-selection → group-chat view rendering, re-run RETEST #3 fully: (a) create TestProj1 via sidebar, (b) click it, (c) verify chat-input appears, (d) send 'HELLO_IN_GROUP', (e) switch to Red's 1-on-1 and verify HELLO_IN_GROUP does NOT appear as a visible message bubble, (f) inspect cc_msgs_agent_red and the project's cc_msgs_* scope key to confirm the message lives ONLY in the project scope. The other 3 retests are now green and should not need re-running unless related code is touched.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE for retest 3 partial: SidebarNavigation.handleCreateProject persists the new project and invokes onSelectProject(newProject.id), but the parent screen that owns the main chat area does not swap to a group-chat view upon that selection — the main area continues to show the empty 'Create Your First Project' landing state. REPRO: 1) Fresh app (localStorage cleared). 2) Click '+ New Group Project'. 3) Enter 'TestProj1' and click Create. 4) Observe that TestProj1 appears in sidebar but main area stays on landing. 5) Click 'TestProj1' in sidebar — still no chat input. EXPECTED: main area renders a group chat with chat-input and chat-send-button testIDs. MITIGATION: ensure the consuming screen (likely ChatHub.tsx) sets activeSection='project' and activeProjectId on select, then renders <ChatMainArea scope={{ type:'project', id: activeProjectId }} /> which already has the chat-input/send testIDs.
